answer:
We are evaluating  
\[
-\frac{1}{n} \log q(X_1, \ldots, X_n) = -\frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \log q(X_i)
\]  
where \(X_i \sim N(0,1)\) and \(q(x)\) is the \(N(1,1)\) density. By the law of large numbers, this converges to  
\[
-\mathbb{E}_{X \sim N(0,1)}[\log q(X)].
\]  
Computing this expectation gives the cross-entropy \(H(p,q) = \frac{1}{2} + \frac{1}{2}\log(2\pi) + \frac{1}{2}\), and subtracting the entropy of \(p\) yields the KL divergence \(D(p\|q) = 0.5\).  

Thus the limit is \(0.5\).

\boxed{C}
